chunk_summary: This section outlines the outpatient workflow for managing feeding difficulties and faltering growth, focusing on assessment, intervention, and follow-up strategies to optimize nutritional and developmental outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the outpatient workflow for addressing feeding difficulties and faltering growth.
Identify key assessment tools and intervention strategies for nutritional management.
Learn follow-up protocols to monitor growth and feeding progress.
Recognize the importance of multidisciplinary collaboration in outpatient care.
key_facts: Faltering growth is defined as weight falling below the 2nd percentile or crossing two major centile lines downward.
Outpatient management often includes dietary assessment, feeding behavior evaluation, and growth monitoring.
Intervention strategies may involve caloric supplementation, behavioral therapy, and caregiver education.
Follow-up intervals are typically every 2-4 weeks initially, depending on severity.
Multidisciplinary teams, including pediatricians, dietitians, and speech therapists, are essential for effective management.
reference_quote: Effective outpatient management of faltering growth requires a structured approach, including regular monitoring and tailored interventions to address underlying causes.
chunk_heading: FEEDING DIFFICULTIES & FALTERING GROWTH;OUTPATIENT DOMAIN WORKFLOW
